_comment_printWidth: Maximum line length before Prettier wraps code. Set to 100 to balance readability with horizontal space usage. This matches the max_line_length setting in .editorconfig.
_comment_tabWidth: Number of spaces per indentation level. Set to 2 to match the project's EditorConfig and TypeScript/React community conventions.
_comment_useTabs: Use spaces instead of tabs for indentation. This ensures consistent rendering across all editors, terminals, and code review interfaces.
_comment_semi: Add semicolons at the end of statements. While JavaScript has automatic semicolon insertion (ASI), explicit semicolons prevent subtle bugs caused by ASI's sometimes unintuitive behavior.
_comment_singleQuote: Use single quotes instead of double quotes for strings. This is the dominant convention in the JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystem and reduces visual noise compared to double quotes.
_comment_quoteProps: Only add quotes around object property names when necessary (i.e., when the property name is not a valid identifier). This reduces visual noise while ensuring valid syntax for properties with special characters.
_comment_jsxSingleQuote: Use double quotes in JSX attributes. This follows the HTML convention (where attribute values use double quotes) and helps visually distinguish JSX from regular JavaScript code.
_comment_trailingComma: Add trailing commas wherever possible (function parameters, array elements, object properties). Trailing commas make version control diffs cleaner by ensuring that adding a new item only shows one changed line instead of two.
_comment_bracketSpacing: Add spaces inside object literal braces. This improves readability by providing visual separation between the braces and the content: { foo: bar } vs {foo: bar}.
_comment_bracketSameLine: Put the closing bracket of multi-line JSX elements on its own line. This makes it easier to see where the opening tag ends and the content begins, especially for components with many props.
_comment_arrowParens: Always include parentheses around arrow function parameters, even when there is only one parameter. This makes it easier to add/remove parameters and provides visual consistency: (x) => x vs x => x.
_comment_endOfLine: Use Unix-style line endings (LF) for all files. This prevents mixed line endings in the repository, which can cause issues with version control diffs and some Unix-based tools.
_comment_htmlWhitespaceSensitivity: Use CSS-based whitespace sensitivity for HTML. This means Prettier will respect the CSS display property of HTML elements when deciding whether whitespace is significant.
_comment_embeddedLanguageFormatting: Automatically format embedded code blocks (like CSS in styled-components or HTML in template literals) using Prettier's formatting rules for that language.
_comment_singleAttributePerLine: Do not force each JSX/HTML attribute onto its own line. Let Prettier decide based on the printWidth setting whether to keep attributes on one line or split them across multiple lines.
